item.title: Building my portfolio site with Hugo and GitHub Pages
item.description:
I wanted a portfolio site that actually looked like something I built rather than something I dragged and dropped together. Most security portfolio sites look identical, a template with a headshot, a skills progress bar, and a contact form. I wanted something cleaner and more intentional, so I built mine from scratch using Hugo and deployed it on GitHub Pages with a custom domain.

This writeup covers how I built it, the problems I ran into, and the security decisions I made along the way.

item.title: About
item.description:
I didn’t take a straight path into cybersecurity.

My original plan was accounting, which I walked away from pretty quickly once I realized it wasn’t for me. After that I spent time trying to break into the electrical trade, didn’t have much luck finding work, and ended up working for a railroad contractor. It paid the bills but I felt like I was just going through the motions.

What changed things was teaching myself how to build a website. I wanted to put together an ecommerce site and started learning UI/UX in the process. That opened a door I didn’t expect. Digging into web development exposed me to the broader world of IT, and somewhere in that process I found cybersecurity. It clicked immediately in a way that nothing else had. I enrolled at UNO shortly after and haven’t looked back.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
The best way to reach me is email. I check it regularly and will get back to you.

eabustamante1@gmail.com

Other Places to Contact me

GitHub – where my code and writeups live.

LinkedIn – for professional connections and opportunities.

I am open to connecting with other security professionals, discussing detection engineering or GRC, or talking about possible internship and job opportunities for Summer 2027 or after graduation in May of 2028.

item.title: PGP
item.description:
If you want to reach me securely you can encrypt your message using my public key below.

Key ID: C40C15BD0B031356

Fingerprint: 25F2 D400 EBA7 D3E9 0861 1CEA C40C 15BD 0B03 1356

To get my key directly from a keyserver run:

gpg --recv-keys C40C15BD0B031356

Or import it manually by copying the block below:

item.title: Projects
item.description:
These are things I am actively working on. Nothing here is listed as finished until it actually is.

Proxmox home lab

Status: in progress, waiting on hardware

Building a dedicated security lab on a Bosgame P3 Lite mini PC running Proxmox. The plan is to run pfSense on the dual 2.5GbE NICs for real WAN/LAN separation, a Wazuh server to collect logs from all lab VMs, a Kali attack machine, an isolated Windows sandbox for malware analysis, and a Ubuntu target with a QEMU ARM environment for low-level work.

item.title: Resume
item.description:
Download PDF

Eduardo Bustamante

Omaha, NE eabustamante1@gmail.com LinkedIn ebustamante.dev
// summary

Cybersecurity student at the University of Nebraska at Omaha pursuing a B.S. in Cybersecurity with a Computer Science minor. Currently working as a SOC Analyst Intern with hands-on exposure to security monitoring, threat identification, and incident documentation. Building toward a career in detection engineering and blue team security operations.

// experience
SOC Analyst Intern University of Nebraska at Omaha, College of IST
Oct 2025 – Present Omaha, NE
  • Participated in the MATRIX Project supporting cybersecurity monitoring initiatives
  • Assisted with security event review, threat identification, and incident documentation
  • Collaborated with technical teams in a professional on-site SOC environment
Landscaper Vlcek Gardens
2024 – 2025 Chapman, NE
  • Performed physically demanding outdoor work with strong attention to detail
  • Demonstrated reliability and time management while balancing full-time academics
Railroad Laborer R & S Track Maintenance Inc.
2023 – 2024 Columbus, NE
  • Assisted with railroad track maintenance following strict safety procedures
  • Worked effectively in high-risk, team-based environments
Walmart Associate Walmart
2022 – 2023 Grand Island, NE
  • Provided customer service in a fast-paced retail environment
  • Managed stocking, inventory, and daily operational tasks
// education
B.S. Cybersecurity University of Nebraska at Omaha
Jan 2025 – May 2028 Omaha, NE
  • Minor in Computer Science
  • Dean's List: Spring 2025, Fall 2025
  • NSA CAE-CO and CAE-CD dual concentrations
  • Fast Track MS program
  • NULLify Cybersecurity Club
B.S. Computer Science University of Nebraska–Lincoln
Aug 2024 – Dec 2024 Lincoln, NE
  • GPA: 3.807
  • Dean's List: Fall 2024
  • OS2G (Open Source and Operating Systems)
// skills
Detection & SIEM Wazuh, Splunk
Languages Python, Bash, C, ARM assembly
Tools Wireshark, Nmap, Ghidra, Git
Frameworks NIST SP 800-30, NIST CSF, CMMC
Operating Systems Kali Linux, Ubuntu, Rocky Linux, Windows
Infrastructure Proxmox, pfSense
